questions.q: On what date was the national Emergency proclaimed in India?
questions.explanation: The national Emergency was declared on 25 June 1975.

questions.q: On what date was the Emergency of 1975-77 formally revoked?
questions.explanation: The Emergency remained in force until it was revoked on 21 March 1977.

questions.q: Who was the Prime Minister of India when the 1975 Emergency was proclaimed?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was the Prime Minister at the time the Emergency was proclaimed in 1975.

questions.q: On what ground was the 1975 Emergency proclaimed?
options: Failure of constitutional machinery in a state
questions.explanation: It was the first and only Emergency proclaimed on the ground of internal disturbance.

questions.q: Who was the President of India who issued the proclamation of Emergency in June 1975?
questions.explanation: President Fakhruddin Ali Ahmed issued the proclamation of Emergency in June 1975.

questions.q: The 1975 proclamation declared which type of constitutional emergency?
questions.explanation: It was a national emergency proclaimed under Article 352 of the Constitution.

questions.q: Roughly how long did the 1975-77 Emergency remain in force?
questions.explanation: Lasting from 25 June 1975 to 21 March 1977, it was in force for about 21 months.

questions.q: A defining feature of the Emergency period was the curtailment of what?
questions.explanation: During the Emergency, civil liberties were curtailed.

questions.q: How many times had a national emergency on the ground of internal disturbance been declared in India as described for this period?
options: It was the first and only such instance
It was the third such instance
It had been declared five times
questions.explanation: It was the first and only Emergency proclaimed on grounds of internal disturbance.

questions.q: In which decade did the Emergency described here take place?
questions.explanation: The Emergency was proclaimed in 1975 and lifted in 1977, within the 1970s.

questions.q: Which Article of the Constitution provides for the proclamation of a national emergency?
questions.explanation: Article 352 provides for the proclamation of a national emergency.

questions.q: The original grounds in Article 352 for a national emergency included war, external aggression, and which third ground that was invoked in 1975?
questions.explanation: The 1975 Emergency was invoked on the ground of internal disturbance, then part of Article 352.

questions.q: Which constitutional amendment passed in 1976 is closely associated with the Emergency period?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment, passed in 1976 during the Emergency, made wide-ranging changes to the Constitution.

questions.q: Which general election held shortly after the Emergency led to a change of government at the Union level?
questions.explanation: The 1977 general election, held after the Emergency was lifted, brought about a change of government.

questions.q: During the Emergency, the term of the Lok Sabha was extended by Parliament. This power to extend the House's life during an emergency derives from which provision?
questions.explanation: A proviso to Article 83 allows Parliament to extend the term of the Lok Sabha during a national emergency.

questions.q: Under the Constitution, during a national emergency the executive power of the Union may give directions to states. This reflects the strengthening of which feature?
options: The federal balance toward the Union
questions.explanation: A national emergency strengthens the Union's authority over the states, tilting the federal balance toward the centre.

questions.q: A national emergency proclamation under Article 352, as required after the constitutional framework was settled, must be approved by which body to remain in force?
questions.explanation: A proclamation of national emergency must be approved by both Houses of Parliament to continue in force.

questions.q: The Emergency of 1975-77 was followed by which committee that examined excesses of the period?
questions.explanation: The Shah Commission was set up after the Emergency to inquire into the events of that period.

questions.q: Which fundamental right's enforcement is most directly affected by Article 359 during a national emergency?
options: The enforcement of certain fundamental rights through the courts
questions.explanation: Article 359 empowers the President to suspend the enforcement of certain fundamental rights through the courts during an emergency.

questions.q: How many earlier national emergencies (on grounds of war or external aggression) had been proclaimed before the 1975 Emergency?
questions.explanation: Two earlier national emergencies, in 1962 and 1971, had been proclaimed on grounds of external aggression before 1975.

questions.q: The 1962 national emergency was proclaimed in connection with which event?
questions.explanation: The 1962 national emergency was proclaimed during the India-China border conflict.

questions.q: The 1971 national emergency coincided with which event?
questions.explanation: The 1971 national emergency was proclaimed in connection with the India-Pakistan war of that year.

questions.q: The 44th Amendment of 1978 replaced the words 'internal disturbance' in Article 352 with which phrase?
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment substituted 'armed rebellion' for 'internal disturbance' as a ground for national emergency.

questions.q: After the 44th Amendment, a proclamation of national emergency requires that the Cabinet's decision be communicated to the President in what form?
options: An oral request from the Prime Minister
A resolution of one House only
A directive from the Supreme Court
A written recommendation of the Cabinet
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment required that the proclamation be based on the written advice of the Cabinet, communicated to the President.

questions.q: Which Supreme Court case decided during the Emergency dealt with the suspension of the right to move courts for enforcement of personal liberty?
questions.explanation: The ADM Jabalpur case (1976) concerned the suspension of access to courts for habeas corpus during the Emergency.

questions.q: After the 44th Amendment, within what period must a proclamation of national emergency be approved by Parliament to remain in force?
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment reduced the period for parliamentary approval of an emergency proclamation to one month.

questions.q: Under provisions strengthened by the 44th Amendment, which two fundamental rights cannot be suspended even during a national emergency?
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment provided that the rights under Articles 20 and 21 cannot be suspended during an emergency.

questions.q: After the 44th Amendment, a continued national emergency must be re-approved by Parliament at intervals of how long?
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment required parliamentary re-approval of a continuing emergency every six months.

questions.q: Which High Court judgment in 1975, concerning the Prime Minister's election, formed part of the immediate backdrop to the Emergency?
questions.explanation: A judgment of the Allahabad High Court on the Prime Minister's election was part of the backdrop to the 1975 Emergency.

questions.q: The 44th Amendment, which reformed emergency provisions, was enacted by the government that came to power after which election?
questions.explanation: The Janata Party government formed after the 1977 election enacted the 44th Amendment in 1978.
